title: Do not add EmptyLayer entries to DiffID
body:
This is an immediate fix for the assertion failure in #684, but it’s not at all clear to me that this is the _right_ fix.

@nalind RFC

comments.body: (See #684 for more discussion; if this PR should ever be merged, I also need to update the commit message.)
(Updated to also handle the other branch appending to `DiffIDs`, and to do the consistency check in all cases.)
@mtrmac The tests want you to make this call into a function.
I think your analysis is correct, and the patch looks good.

> @mtrmac The tests want you to make this call into a function.

Hopefully resolved by consolidating two duplicate instances of the same code.  (Extracting the code into a function is still an option, at least it will be simpler code to extract.)
FYI @pixdrift, in case you didn't get pinged about this one.

@mtrmac I think this needs more adjusting unfortunately:

```
# buildah from alpine
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:ff3a5c916c92643ff77519ffa742d3ec61b7f591b6b7504599d95a4a41134e28

Copying config sha256:3fd9065eaf02feaf94d68376da52541925650b81698c53c6824d92ff63f98353
 1.48 KiB / 1.48 KiB [======================================================] 0s
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
alpine-working-container

# buildah commit alpine-working-container myalpine
error committing container "alpine-working-container" to "myalpine": error copying layers and metadata: Error initializing source containers-storage:alpine-working-container: Internal error: 2 layers vs. 3 history items
```

Similar issues in the tests that are failing.

and if I turn of the three error checks in the code that make sure the sizes all match up, the manifest is still not looking happy fwiw.

```

@rhatdan asked me to check @mitrs reproducer from #684 
```
skopeo copy --format v2s1 docker://mysql:5.5 dir:t
img=$(buildah from dir:t)
buildah inspect $img
python -mjson.tool t/manifest.json |less
buildah commit $img test-dir
```
That worked without an issue....

And if I continue on using @pixdrift's example and to the commit that was failing, it now cores.

```

Just in case, here's @pixdrift 's reproducer
```
# Setup a private registry
docker run -d -p 5000:5000 --name registry registry:latest

# Pull a container image from docker.io and push it to private registry
newcontainer=$(buildah from alpine)
buildah commit ${newcontainer} myalpine
buildah push --format v2s1 --tls-verify=false myalpine docker://localhost:5000/alpinev2s1

# Use buildah to pull from the private registry and inspect the result to see broken metadata
brokencontainer=$(buildah from --tls-verify=false docker://localhost:5000/alpinev2s1)
buildah inspect ${brokencontainer} | grep Manifest

# Generate error by attempting to commit image
buildah commit ${brokencontainer} broken-layers
error committing container "alpinev2s1-working-container" to "broken-layers": error copying layers and metadata: Error initializing source containers-storage:alpinev2s1-working-container: internal error: history lists 1 non-empty layers, but we have 4 layers on disk
```

> ```
> # buildah from alpine
> # buildah commit alpine-working-container myalpine
> ```

Ah, my mistake.  This PR modifies the code to expect the results of pulling v2s1 (where empty layers physically exist), but in v2s2 they don’t exist (and it would be non-trivial to physically create them, and anyway we don’t really want to pay the price).  So let’s try the opposite, when pulling, skip empty layers: https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/pull/775 .
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 6c4bef7) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
